Compare content from in-memory string returned by `git.getFileContent({ workspaceId, ref, relPath })` (left = HEAD/INDEX); right uses existing `fs.readFile` for working-tree (or git show :ref for INDEX). Diff tab subscribes to `fs.changed` + `git.statusChanged` to refetch on change. Reject: inline diff in panel (couples lifecycle, can't multi-diff), single-file editor with decorations only (doesn't show against HEAD), virtual `git://` URI scheme (custom protocol handler + cache invalidation = large surface for v1 with no benefit over a typed call), temp files (cleanup risk on crash, cross-platform path quirks). Risks: large files → cap getFileContent at ~10MB, typed error → renderer shows "too large to diff"; binary files → use existing binary-detect.ts → render placeholder; refresh race → version each side (mtime/oid), only apply newer responses; tab leaks → existing tab cleanup by workspaceId already covers. Files: src/shared/types/tab.ts (extend union to include "editor.diff" with DiffTabPayload), src/renderer/components/editor/{diff-tab.tsx, diff-content-loader.ts}, src/renderer/state/stores/tabs.ts (openDiffTab).

issues.decision:
**Open a Monaco `DiffEditor` in a new editor tab with a new tab subtype `editor.diff`. Compare content comes from in-memory strings: left side via `git.getFileContent({ workspaceId, ref, relPath })`, right side via the existing `fs.readFile` (working-tree) or `git.getFileContent` for the index. No virtual `git://` URI scheme. No temp files. The diff tab subscribes to `fs.changed` + `git.statusChanged` and refetches on changes affecting its `relPath`.**

Per the architect's analysis. The tab subtype is a discriminated extension on `TabMeta`, not a string convention — added to `src/shared/types/tab.ts`. `DiffTabPayload = { workspaceId; relPath; leftRef: "HEAD" | "INDEX" | string; rightRef: "WORKING" | "INDEX"; oldRelPath?: string }`. Renames open with both paths visible in the tab title and labelled left/right.

**Why a new tab and not an inline panel diff.** vscode's full diff editor is the user's mental model; a 240–360 px panel cannot host a usable side-by-side Monaco instance, and an inline single-pane diff is materially worse than what we already have when the user clicks a regular file. Reusing the existing tab system also means the diff tab inherits workspace-scoped tab cleanup (closeAllForWorkspace) for free.

**Why typed call instead of `git://` URI scheme.** A custom Electron protocol handler (`protocol.handle("git", …)`) would have to wire URI parsing, content loading, cache invalidation on `git.statusChanged`, and probably a custom `model.uri` strategy for Monaco. That's hundreds of lines of plumbing for v1 with no advantage over a typed `getFileContent` call that returns a string. The architect's call is correct: small typed surface > large generic surface, especially when the only consumers are the diff tab and (future) the editor's "go to git original" command.

**Live-update strategy.** Diff tab keeps a per-side version token (`leftSideOid`, `rightSideMtime`). On `fs.changed` containing `relPath`, refetch the right side. On `git.statusChanged` for the workspace, refetch the left side (HEAD/INDEX content can move when the user commits or stages). Apply a fetch only if the response token is newer than what's currently displayed — a slow response from a stale fetch never overwrites a fresh one.

**Edge cases.**
- *Large files* — `getFileContent` caps at 10 MB and throws a typed error; renderer shows "File too large to diff." Configurable later, not in v1.
- *Binary files* — main calls existing `binary-detect.ts`; if either side is binary, return `{ binary: true }` and the renderer renders a placeholder with file size, not a Monaco model.
- *File deleted on the right* — left renders the HEAD content, right renders empty with a "(deleted)" placeholder.
- *File added on the right* — left renders empty with a "(new file)" placeholder, right renders the working content.
- *Renames* — title shows `oldRelPath → relPath`; left side fetches `getFileContent({ ref: "HEAD", relPath: oldRelPath })`.

**Rejected alternatives.** Inline diff in the SCM panel (couples lifecycle to the panel, can't open multiple diffs); single-file editor with gutter decorations only (doesn't show "what changed against HEAD" — the core SCM task); virtual `git://` URI scheme (large protocol-handler + cache-coherence surface, no v1 benefit); writing temp files (cleanup risk on crash, cross-platform path quirks, makes workspace deletion non-atomic).

**Risks & mitigations.** Refresh races (file changes mid-diff and two responses arrive out of order) → version each side, only apply newer; tab leaks on workspace removal → existing tab cleanup handles by workspaceId; diff editor instantiation cost (Monaco's DiffEditor is heavier than the editor) → lazy-load the diff tab component, share the Monaco instance from `@monaco-editor/react`.

**Files this decision creates:** `src/shared/types/tab.ts` (extend the union to include `"editor.diff"` with `DiffTabPayload`), `src/renderer/components/editor/diff-tab.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/editor/diff-content-loader.ts` (the dual-side fetch + version-tracking hook), `src/renderer/state/operations/tabs.ts` gains `openDiffTab(workspaceId, relPath, leftRef, rightRef, oldRelPath?)`. The `git.getFileContent` call is added in issue 4's contract.

issues.title: Renderer state and UI structure — zustand store shape, panel sections (commit input + groups + file rows), workspace-cleanup hookup

analysis.summary: Panel layout (top-down): mode-bar (existing) → 32px GitHeader (uppercase "SOURCE CONTROL" caption + refresh icon + overflow), → GitCommitInput (autosize textarea ABOVE groups, ⌘Enter triggers, full-width split Commit button below with Amend/Commit & Push in chevron) → group sections (only non-empty groups shown — Merge/Staged/Changes/Untracked, 28px headers with chevron+label+count+hover-revealed group actions) → 28px GitBranchBar footer (branch name truncate-end, ↓N ↑M only when non-zero, Sync button consolidates push+pull, more-menu retains explicit Push/Pull/Fetch). File rows: 24px tall (matches search/file-tree row), 4 zones — status badge + filename (front-truncated via direction:rtl) + hover/focus-revealed inline actions + trailing slot. Default click = open Monaco DiffEditor tab; second click = focus existing tab (no toggle); reveal-in-tree only via right-click (no surprise side effects). Status badge token map: M/T→warning, A/?→success, D→destructive, R/C→muted-foreground, !→destructive+bg/10 (letter is primary signifier, color secondary for a11y). Empty/non-repo state: centered "NOT A GIT REPOSITORY" caption + Initialize Repository pill button. Components to create: GitPanel, GitHeader, GitMoreMenu, GitCommitInput, GitCommitButton, GitGroup, GitGroupHeader, GitFileRow, GitStatusBadge, GitBranchBar, GitBranchPopover, GitInlineBanner, GitEmptyState, GitLoadingSkeleton, useGitSession, useGitOpHotkey, confirmDiscardDialog. Keyboard contract: Tab order header→commit→groups→rows; Enter=open diff, Space=toggle stage, Backspace=discard with confirm, ⌘Enter=commit. Warnings: text-success contrast on bg-frosted-veil-strong must be measured at impl time; hover-only actions must also reveal on row focus; sync conflates push/pull → keep explicit entries in more-menu. Out of v1: hunk staging, blame, file-history graph, branch graph, conflict resolution UI in panel, tag/remote management, PR integration, commit-message linting, global status bar.

issues.decision:
**Adopt the designer's full layout: 32 px GitHeader → autosize GitCommitInput with split Commit button → only-non-empty groups in order Merge/Staged/Changes/Untracked → 28 px GitBranchBar footer. Renderer state lives in a new `useGitStore` zustand store keyed by workspaceId, mirroring `useSearchStore`'s session-map shape and registering `closeAllForWorkspace` with `registerWorkspaceCleanup`.**

**Layout specifics (per designer Q1–Q5).**
- *GitHeader (32 px)*: uppercase "SOURCE CONTROL" caption (`text-small-label uppercase tracking-[2.4px]`) + refresh icon button + overflow icon. More-menu carries Stash, Stash pop, Fetch, Pull, Push, Checkout…, Create branch…, Initialize / open in terminal, Discard all (with confirm).
- *GitCommitInput (above groups)*: 3-line autosize textarea, full-width split button below — primary `Commit`, chevron offers `Amend last commit`, `Commit & Push`, `Commit Staged`. ⌘Enter from anywhere in the panel triggers primary. Empty/no-staged/committing states per Q2.
- *Group headers (28 px)*: chevron + label + count + hover/focus-revealed group-level actions (Stage all / Unstage all / Discard all). Empty groups hidden entirely.
- *File rows (24 px)*: status badge (16 px) + RTL-truncated path (`direction:rtl;text-align:left;unicode-bidi:plaintext`) + hover/focus-revealed inline actions + tooltip with full path + `aria-label` carrying the full path for screen readers. Default click opens the diff tab; right-click menu offers Open File, Reveal in File Tree, Stage/Unstage, Discard.
- *GitBranchBar (28 px footer)*: branch name (truncate-end) · `↓N ↑M` only when non-zero · button label adapts: `Sync` (both), `Push` (only ahead), `Pull` (only behind), disabled with "Up to date" tooltip when zero.

**Status badge token map (per designer):** M/T → `text-warning` · A/? → `text-success` · D → `text-destructive` · R/C → `text-muted-foreground` · ! → `text-destructive` + subtle `bg-destructive/10`. Single-letter glyph is the primary signifier; color is supplementary so colorblind users aren't dependent on hue. **Pre-impl verification:** measure `text-success` contrast on `bg-frosted-veil-strong` (the row hover background) — if it falls below 4.5:1, drop hover background opacity for status-bearing rows or shift status to bold weight (designer's flagged warning).

**Keyboard contract.** Tab order: header buttons → commit input → commit button → group headers → file rows. Within a row: Enter opens the diff, Space toggles staged/unstaged, Backspace discards (with confirm), ⌘Enter commits from anywhere. Hover-revealed inline actions also become visible on row focus. Esc from commit input blurs to previous focus; Esc on a row clears row selection. `confirmDiscardDialog` uses `role="alertdialog"` with default focus on Cancel for destructive actions.

**State shape.**
```ts
interface GitSession {
  repoInfo: RepoInfo;          // detecting | non-repo | repo
  status: GitStatus | null;    // last broadcast
  statusFetching: boolean;
  branchInfo: { current: string; ahead: number; behind: number; upstream: string | null } | null;
  commitDraft: string;         // persisted (issue 9)
  expandedGroups: Record<"merge" | "staged" | "working" | "untracked", boolean>;
  inFlightOp: { kind: "commit" | "push" | "pull" | …; startedAt: number } | null;
  lastError: { kind: string; message: string; details?: string } | null;
}
const useGitStore = create<{ sessions: Map<workspaceId, GitSession>; … }>(…);
```

The store subscribes to `git.statusChanged` and `git.repoInfoChanged` once at module init (single IPC subscription, fan-out by workspaceId). It registers `closeAllForWorkspace` with `registerWorkspaceCleanup` so a removed workspace's session disappears together with its in-flight op AbortController (mirrors `useSearchStore`).

**Components to create (designer-named):** `GitPanel`, `GitHeader`, `GitMoreMenu`, `GitCommitInput`, `GitCommitButton`, `GitGroup`, `GitGroupHeader`, `GitFileRow`, `GitStatusBadge`, `GitBranchBar`, `GitBranchPopover`, `GitInlineBanner`, `GitEmptyState`, `GitLoadingSkeleton`. Hooks: `useGitSession(workspaceId)`, `useGitOpHotkey()`, plus reuse of the existing `useLoaderDelay` (no new git-specific delay hook). Plus `confirmDiscardDialog`.

**Mounting wiring.** `FilesPanel` already routes `mode === "git"` to `ComingSoon`; this decision replaces that branch with `<GitPanel workspaceId={activeWorkspace.id} />`.

**Rejected alternatives.** Branch chrome in the header (fails the legibility check at 240 px when squeezed against refresh + overflow); show all four groups always (predictable but wastes vertical real estate when 2–3 are typically empty); single-button Commit (loses the Amend/Commit-and-Push affordance for power users — split button costs little and earns its width); inline diff in the panel (rejected upstream in issue 6); auto-reveal file in tree on diff click (surprise side-effect on a different panel — violates locality); a separate global status bar (out of v1 scope, the panel-local footer covers branch/sync needs).

**Out of v1 (designer):** inline diff hovers on rows, hunk staging, blame, history graph, conflict-resolution UI in the panel (we send users to the diff tab; resolved-then-staged is the supported flow), tag/remote management, PR integration, commit-message linting / Conventional Commits enforcement, global status bar, new accent colors or typefaces.

issues.title: Authentication for push/pull — defer to v2 with surface-error stub, or include minimal askpass
issues.decision:
**Defer interactive auth (askpass / credential helper UI) to v2. v1 ships push/pull/fetch as ordinary git invocations: they succeed when credentials are already available (HTTPS cached creds, SSH agent, OS keychain helper) and surface stderr as a typed error when they are not. No GIT_ASKPASS IPC server, no in-app credential prompt, no SSH passphrase modal.**

**Why defer.** Research showed vscode's askpass mechanism is ~500 LOC across `askpass.ts`, `askpassManager.ts`, and a small Node.js IPC server invoked via `GIT_ASKPASS` env, plus a separate SSH askpass for `SSH_ASKPASS`/`SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE`. Building a correct askpass is on the same order of complexity as the entire SCM panel UI we are designing in v1; it is not a single afternoon. Most users on macOS/Linux already have either an OS keychain helper (osxkeychain) or `ssh-agent` running, in which case push/pull "just work" with zero in-app interaction. The fraction of users who need interactive credentials in v1 is small; for them, the in-app terminal already exists and works perfectly.

**v1 behavior on auth failure.** Spawn child with `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, `GIT_ASKPASS=echo` (returns empty so git fails immediately rather than blocking on stdin), and `SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE=force`/`SSH_ASKPASS=echo` for SSH. When git exits non-zero with stderr matching auth patterns ("could not read Username", "Permission denied (publickey)", "fatal: Authentication failed", "remote: Invalid username or password"), the IPC handler returns a typed `GitError` with `kind: "auth"`. The renderer's GitInlineBanner shows: "Authentication required. Set up credentials in Terminal: `git push` once to cache them." with a `Open Terminal` action that creates a terminal tab in the workspace. This is the minimum-viable teach-the-user flow — explicit, recoverable, no false claims of capability.

**Push/pull are still in v1's op set.** They are not gated behind auth — they run, they succeed for cached-credential users, they fail with a clear error otherwise. The ratio of "useful for the majority" vs. "blocks the minority into a workaround" is correct.

**Rejected alternatives.**
1. *Include minimal askpass in v1* — would mean spawning a small Node IPC server that listens on a Unix socket (or named pipe on Windows) and routes to a renderer modal. The complexity (server lifecycle, transport security, modal UX, credential persistence policy, SSH passphrase vs. HTTPS password vs. 2FA token disambiguation) materially threatens v1 scope.
2. *Hide push/pull until auth is configured* — discoverability hit; users wouldn't know the panel can do it; we'd then need a separate "configure git" flow which is its own UI.
3. *Pop a system-style password dialog from main with `dialog.showInputBox`* — Electron has no such API by default; we'd be building a renderer modal anyway. Same problem as (1) with a worse UX.
4. *Spawn `git` with the user's terminal attached so prompts work* — feasible only because we have a PTY host, but conflates SCM operations with terminal sessions and breaks our serial queue + abort model. Out of architecture.

**Risks & mitigations.** Users on Windows without Git Credential Manager configured will see auth failures every push → the Open Terminal CTA in the inline banner runs `git push` in PTY where Git Credential Manager *can* prompt and cache. After credentials cache, a subsequent in-app push from the panel succeeds with no extra UI. SSH key-with-passphrase users without `ssh-agent` running will see Permission denied → same Open Terminal CTA, same recovery path. Documented as known v1 limitation in the panel's onboarding tooltip ("First push may need terminal — credentials cache after").

**v2 follow-on.** When askpass is built, the IPC contract added in issue 4 needs no change; only main-side process spawning and a new renderer modal are added. The decision is forward-compatible.

issues.title: Per-workspace persistence — what (if anything) to persist (commit message draft, expanded groups, last fetched, …) and how it integrates with WorkspaceStorage migrations
issues.decision:
**Persist exactly two pieces of per-workspace state for the SCM panel: the unsubmitted commit message draft, and the user's expand/collapse state for the four groups. Add one new SQLite migration (version 3) to `WorkspaceStorage` for both. Nothing else is persisted in v1 — repo info, status, branch info are all derived state and are recomputed on panel open.**

**Why these two and only these two.**
1. *Commit message draft* — a partially-typed message that disappears on workspace switch or app restart is the single most user-hostile bug an SCM panel can have. Persisting per workspace also handles the multi-workspace case correctly: a draft for repo A doesn't leak into repo B. Trivially implemented as a single string row.
2. *Group expand state* — the user's preference (e.g., always-collapsed Untracked because the workspace has many generated files) should survive sessions to match the file-tree expand state pattern (`expanded_paths` table, migration v2). Stored as a small JSON blob since the schema is a fixed map of four booleans.

**Why nothing else.**
- *Repo info / status / branch / ahead-behind* — all derived from git itself; persisting them creates two sources of truth and a stale-data window on app restart (status will be wrong until the first refresh anyway). The 100–300 ms cost of running `git status` on panel open is acceptable; users expect freshness from a SCM panel, not "what we knew yesterday."
- *Last fetched timestamp* — implies a "fetch every N minutes" auto-fetch loop, which is out of v1 scope. Add when auto-fetch lands.
- *Last commit SHA / log cache* — `git.log` is a stream and the diff tab is the only consumer in v1; caching here optimizes nothing.
- *Resolved git binary path* — process-level singleton, not per-workspace; lives in memory only (re-resolved on next launch).

**Migration shape (added to `WORKSPACE_DB_MIGRATIONS` in `src/main/storage/workspace-storage.ts`):**
```ts
{
  version: 3,
  up: (db) => {
    db.exec(`
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS git_panel_state (
        key   TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
        value TEXT NOT NULL
      );
    `);
    // keys: "commitDraft" -> string, "expandedGroups" -> JSON.stringify({merge,staged,working,untracked}: boolean)
  },
}
```
Single key/value table over two dedicated columns because the schema may grow in v2 (e.g., last selected branch in the popover) and a kv table avoids further migrations. Two `WorkspaceStorage` accessor pairs land alongside `getExpandedPaths`/`setExpandedPaths`: `getGitPanelState(id)` / `setGitPanelState(id, state)`. Renderer reads via two new `git` channel calls? — **no**, persistence is set/get for the panel only and doesn't need a dedicated channel. Wire instead through the existing `appState`-style pattern is overkill too. Cleanest: extend the existing `fs` channel? No — git's panel state is git's responsibility. **Final**: two thin calls on the `git` channel: `getPanelState({ workspaceId })` and `setPanelState({ workspaceId, ... })`. Added to issue 4's contract during implementation, not a separate channel.

**Rejected alternatives.**
- *Persist nothing and store draft in zustand only* — drafts vanish on app restart; users will type a long message, command-tab away, come back to nothing. This is the bug we are explicitly preventing.
- *Persist everything (status, branch, last log)* — invites stale-data bugs and contributes nothing to UX since status must be re-run anyway.
- *Use a separate file (`git-panel.json`) per workspace next to `workspace.json`* — adds another recovery dump path and another file to clean up; the migration table is the established mechanism.
- *Two dedicated tables (one for draft, one for groups)* — overengineers a 2-key kv use case.

**Risks & mitigations.** Schema growth in v2 (last branch, search filter, etc.) → kv shape absorbs new keys without migration. Concurrent write storms (every keystroke writes draft) → renderer debounces draft persistence at 500 ms idle, just like `setFilesPanelWidth`'s debounced persist. Stale draft for a deleted-and-recreated branch → the draft is per workspace, not per branch; users are free to clear it. Acceptable tradeoff vs. the complexity of branch-keyed drafts.

**Files this decision touches:** `src/main/storage/workspace-storage.ts` (migration v3 + two accessors), `src/main/ipc/channels/git/repo-handlers.ts` (or a sibling `state-handlers.ts`) for the two thin getPanelState/setPanelState calls wired to the storage methods.

plan.research_summary: VSCode git extension structure mapped (model.ts/repository.ts/git.ts/operation.ts/staging.ts/commands.ts/decorationProvider.ts/quickDiffProvider.ts/askpass*.ts) — repos are auto-detected from .git/, status held as Index/WorkingTree/Merge/Untracked groups, ~40 operations exposed but a v1 of 10 (stage/unstage/discard/commit/pull/push/fetch/checkout/create-branch/stash) is reasonable, status codes M/A/D/R/C + U + ! for conflicts, diffs served via virtual git:// FS provider that calls `git show` + `git diff` on demand, askpass IPC is ~500 LOC and reasonable to defer. Current nexus-code patterns mapped: WorkspaceManager + WorkspaceContext lifecycle in src/main/workspace, registerWorkspaceCleanup central registry in renderer, ipc-contract.ts uses call/listen/stream split with workspaceId UUID in every method, SearchPanel illustrates the ipcStream pattern with AbortController-per-workspace and zustand session map keyed by workspaceId, FilesPanel already has a "git" mode showing ComingSoon — the UI store key is `filesPanelModes`, WorkspaceStorage migration list in src/main/storage/workspace-storage.ts is the place to add a git-related table (version 3+), lsp-host/pty-host show the precedent for spawn-on-demand utility processes with pendingCalls request queue. file-watcher.ts is disposed per-workspace via wrappedBroadcast in main/index.ts when workspace is removed.

tasks.title: ipc-contract `stream` 카테고리 + 추론 타입 도입
tasks.context:
ipcStream 프리미티브의 타입 anchor 신설. 모든 후속 단계가 이 카테고리에 의존하므로 가장 먼저 진행. `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts`에 새 procedure descriptor와 factory를, `src/renderer/ipc/types.ts`에 추론 유틸을 추가. 기존 call/listen 사용처에는 영향 없음.</context>
<parameter name="approach">1. `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts`에 `interface StreamProcedure<A,P,R> { args: A; progress: P; result: R; }` 정의.
2. 같은 파일에 `function stream<A,P,R>(args, progress, result): StreamProcedure<A,P,R>` factory.
3. `InferProgress<T>`, `InferComplete<T>` 추론 타입 추가 (기존 InferArgs/InferReturn 옆에).
4. 기존 channel definition 객체가 `stream?: Record<string, StreamProcedure>` 필드를 선택적으로 가질 수 있도록 타입 허용.
5. `src/renderer/ipc/types.ts`에 `StreamChannels`, `StreamMethods<C>`, `StreamArgs<C,M>`, `StreamProgress<C,M>`, `StreamComplete<C,M>` 타입 유틸 추가.
6. 본 단계에서는 ipc-contract의 fs.stream 등록은 하지 않음 (Task 6의 search 마이그레이션 단계에서). 타입 시스템만 준비.
tasks.acceptance:
- `bun tsc --noEmit` 통과.
- `StreamProcedure`, `stream()` factory, `InferProgress`/`InferComplete`이 contract에 export되고, `StreamChannels`/`StreamMethods<C>`/`StreamArgs/Progress/Complete<C,M>`이 ipc/types에 export됨.
- 기존 `InferArgs`/`InferReturn` 동작 변경 없음.
- biome lint 통과.
tasks.risk: 사소한 위험: 기존 channel definition 타입을 확장할 때 `as const` 추론이 깨질 수 있음. 해결: `stream` 필드를 선택적으로 두고 사용처에서 점진 도입.
tasks.approach:
1. `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts`에 `interface StreamProcedure<A,P,R> { args: A; progress: P; result: R; }` 정의.
2. 같은 파일에 `function stream<A,P,R>(args, progress, result): StreamProcedure<A,P,R>` factory.
3. `InferProgress<T>`, `InferComplete<T>` 추론 타입 추가 (기존 InferArgs/InferReturn 옆에).
4. 기존 channel definition 객체가 `stream?: Record<string, StreamProcedure>` 필드를 선택적으로 가질 수 있도록 타입 허용.
5. `src/renderer/ipc/types.ts`에 `StreamChannels`, `StreamMethods<C>`, `StreamArgs<C,M>`, `StreamProgress<C,M>`, `StreamComplete<C,M>` 타입 유틸 추가.
6. 본 단계에서는 ipc-contract의 fs.stream 등록은 하지 않음 (Task 6의 search 마이그레이션 단계에서). 타입 시스템만 준비.

result.summary: Added exported stream procedure descriptor/factory and progress/complete inference types in the shared IPC contract, plus renderer stream channel/method/args/progress/complete derivation types. Verified with bun tsc --noEmit and Biome checks on touched files.

tasks.title: preload bridge에 streamStart invoke + streamEvent demux 추가
tasks.context: renderer가 main의 stream을 호출할 때 사용할 transport 표면. 기존 preload bridge(`window.ipc.call/cancel/listen/off`)는 그대로 두고, 새 streaming 경로를 별도로 추가. streamId 키 demux Map은 기존 listeners Map과 무관하게 동작.
tasks.acceptance:
- 기존 `window.ipc.{call, cancel, listen, off}` 시그니처와 동작 무변경.
- `window.ipc.streamStart(channel, method, args)`가 `Promise<{ streamId: string }>`을 반환.
- `window.ipc.onStreamEvent(streamId, cb)`가 demux 등록 + unregister 함수 반환.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` 통과.
tasks.approach:
1. `src/preload/index.ts`에 `streamListeners: Map<string, (event: StreamEvent) => void>` 추가 (key=streamId).
2. `ipcRenderer.on("ipc:streamEvent", (_event, payload: { streamId, kind, data }) => { streamListeners.get(streamId)?.(payload); })` 단일 demux 구독 추가.
3. `ipcApi`에 새 메서드 추가:
   - `streamStart(channelName, method, args): Promise<{ streamId: string }>` → `ipcRenderer.invoke("ipc:streamStart", channelName, method, args)`.
   - `onStreamEvent(streamId, callback)` → streamListeners에 등록, 반환값은 unregister 함수.
4. 기존 `cancel(requestId)`을 그대로 streamId 취소에도 재사용 (router 측에서 동일 cancel 라우팅).
5. unknown streamId 수신 시 `console.warn("[ipcStream] unknown streamId", streamId)` 한 줄.
tasks.risk: contextBridge 노출 객체 변경은 preload 재빌드가 필요. 기존 ambient 타입 선언(`global.d.ts` 등)도 함께 업데이트해야 함.

result.summary: Added preload streamStart invoke wrapper, streamId-keyed streamEvent demux, unknown-stream warning, unregister support, and ambient window.ipc typings without changing existing call/cancel/listen/off behavior. Verified by engineer with bun tsc --noEmit and Biome lint on touched files.

tasks.title: main router에 stream 등록·실행 (sender-targeted send + AbortController + zod 검증)
tasks.context: streamId 권위 발급과 AsyncGenerator 실행을 담당하는 transport 핵심. 기존 `ipcMain.handle("ipc:call")`/`broadcast` 경로는 무변경. 새 `ipc:streamStart` invoke와 sender-targeted `ipc:streamEvent` send 경로를 별도 추가. 도메인 코드는 streamId를 모름.
tasks.acceptance:
- `register("fs", { stream: { searchText: handler } })` 호출이 타입 안전하게 수용됨.
- ipc:streamStart invoke가 streamId를 즉시 반환, generator가 백그라운드에서 spawn.
- generator yield → 호출자 webContents에만 streamEvent 송신 (다른 webContents에 송신 안 됨).
- generator throw → kind:"error" 이벤트 송신.
- generator return → kind:"complete" 이벤트 송신.
- AbortSignal abort → generator.return() 호출 + 더 이상 progress 송신 안 됨.
- progress payload zod 검증 실패 시 kind:"error" 송신.
- 기존 broadcast/listen/call 경로 동작 무변경.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` 통과.
tasks.approach:
1. `src/main/ipc/router.ts`의 `register()` 시그니처를 확장: `def: { call, listen, stream? }`. stream handler는 `(args, ctx) => AsyncGenerator<TProgress, TComplete>` 형태.
2. `pendingStreamControllers: Map<string, AbortController>` 별도 추가 (기존 pendingCallControllers와 분리).
3. `ipcMain.handle("ipc:streamStart", async (event, channelName, method, args) => {...})`:
   - streamId = `crypto.randomUUID()` (main 단독 권위)
   - AbortController 생성 후 controller를 pendingStreamControllers에 등록
   - 즉시 `{ streamId }` 반환 (이후 send는 비동기로 진행)
   - `void runStream(event.sender, channelName, method, args, streamId, controller.signal)` 비동기 spawn
4. `runStream` 함수가 generator 실행:
   - `validateArgs`로 args zod 검증
   - generator 인스턴스 생성
   - `for await (const progress of gen)`: progress schema로 zod 검증 후 `event.sender.send("ipc:streamEvent", { streamId, kind: "progress", data: progress })` (sender-targeted)
   - generator return → complete schema 검증 후 `kind: "complete"` 송신
   - throw → `kind: "error"` 송신 (AbortError 포함)
   - finally: `pendingStreamControllers.delete(streamId)`
5. `ipcMain.on("ipc:cancel")` 핸들러를 확장하여 streamId도 처리: pendingStreamControllers에서 찾으면 `controller.abort()` → generator의 next iteration이 throw AbortError → kind:error 송신 후 정리.
6. `src/main/index.ts`에 변경 없음. 기존 `setupRouter()` 호출에서 stream 경로도 함께 등록되도록 router.ts 내부에서 처리.
tasks.risk: AsyncGenerator의 `return()` 호출 후 finally 블록 실행 보장은 표준이지만 generator 내부에서 try/finally 누락 시 cleanup 누락 가능 — 도메인 코드에서 신경쓰지 않도록 router가 try/finally로 cleanup 책임을 흡수해야 함. zod 검증 hot-path 비용은 search batch 규모(50 files / 200 matches per batch)에선 무시 가능.

result.summary: Extended the main IPC router with optional stream handlers, ipc:streamStart registration, main-issued streamId lifecycle, sender-targeted ipc:streamEvent progress/complete/error events, zod validation from the shared contract, and AbortController cancellation while preserving existing call/broadcast behavior. Verified by engineer with bun tsc --noEmit, Biome check on router, and existing IPC tests.

tasks.title: renderer ipcStream 클라이언트 구현 (`{ promise, onProgress }` + AbortSignal)
tasks.context: 렌더러 측 ipcStream 함수가 store/컴포넌트에서 호출될 표면. preload bridge(Task 2)와 main router(Task 3)가 준비된 후, 둘을 잇는 도메인 친화 API를 제공. 기존 `ipcCall` 시그니처는 그대로 두고 별도 함수로 추가.
tasks.acceptance:
- `ipcStream(channel, method, args, { signal })` 호출이 `{ promise, onProgress }` 반환.
- onProgress 콜백은 progress 이벤트 수신 시마다 동기 호출됨.
- promise는 complete payload로 resolve, error payload로 reject.
- AbortSignal abort 시 `window.ipc.cancel(streamId)` 호출 + promise는 AbortError로 reject.
- onProgress의 unregister 함수 호출 후 더 이상 콜백 호출 안 됨.
- 타입 추론: 채널·메서드 명에서 args/progress/complete 타입이 자동 추론됨.
- 기존 `ipcCall` 동작 무변경.
tasks.approach:
1. `src/renderer/ipc/client.ts`에 `ipcStream<C, M>` 함수 추가:
```ts
export interface IpcStreamHandle<TProgress, TComplete> {
  promise: Promise<TComplete>;
  onProgress(cb: (data: TProgress) => void): () => void;
}

export function ipcStream<C extends StreamChannels, M extends StreamMethods<C>>(
  channel: C, method: M,
  args: StreamArgs<C, M>,
  opts?: { signal?: AbortSignal },
): IpcStreamHandle<StreamProgress<C, M>, StreamComplete<C, M>>;
```
2. 구현:
   - `streamStart` invoke 호출 → streamId 반환받음
   - progress callbacks 배열 보유, `onProgress(cb)` → 배열에 추가, unregister 함수 반환
   - 내부적으로 `window.ipc.onStreamEvent(streamId, ev => {...})` 등록:
     - kind:progress → 모든 progress callbacks 호출
     - kind:complete → resolve(data) + onStreamEvent unregister
     - kind:error → reject(new Error(message)) + unregister
   - signal.aborted 시 즉시 cancel + reject(AbortError)
   - signal.addEventListener("abort", () => { window.ipc.cancel(streamId); reject(AbortError); }, { once: true })
   - promise resolve/reject finally에서 onStreamEvent unregister 자동
3. JSDoc에 명시: "onProgress는 promise를 await 하기 전에 등록해야 한다. 그렇지 않으면 등록 직전 도착한 progress가 누락될 수 있다."
4. `useIpcStream` React hook은 본 사이클 scope 외 (Issue 1 결정).
tasks.risk: onProgress 등록 타이밍이 promise await 이후라 첫 batch가 누락될 가능성 → API 계약(JSDoc)으로 명시. microtask 트릭은 visibility 침해라 도입하지 않음.

result.summary: Added the typed renderer ipcStream client returning { promise, onProgress }, with progress callback registration/unregistration, complete/error settlement, serialized error conversion, AbortSignal cancellation including pre-start abort handling, and cleanup while leaving ipcCall/ipcListen unchanged. Verified by engineer with bun tsc --noEmit and Biome check on the touched file.

tasks.title: ipcStream 프리미티브 단위 테스트 (router-stream + client-stream)
tasks.context: Task 3·4의 transport 코드가 lifecycle 모든 분기(start/progress/complete/error/abort/zod 검증)에서 정확히 동작하는지 검증. bun:test 환경에서 electron의 webContents/ipcMain·window.ipc는 mock.module로 leaf-only stub. memory의 pattern-bun-mock-conventions 규약 준수.
tasks.acceptance:
- `bun test tests/unit/main/ipc/router-stream.test.ts`: 모든 시나리오 (yield/return/throw/abort/zod-fail/sender-target) 통과.
- `bun test tests/unit/renderer/ipc/client-stream.test.ts`: 모든 시나리오 (progress/complete/error/abort/unregister/wrong-streamId) 통과.
- 두 테스트 파일이 다른 테스트와 독립적으로 (process-global mock 오염 없이) 실행됨.
tasks.approach:
1. **`tests/unit/main/ipc/router-stream.test.ts`** (신규):
   - electron의 `ipcMain`/`webContents` mock을 mock.module로 stub
   - `register("test", { stream: { ping: handler } })` 후 `ipcMain.handle("ipc:streamStart")`를 직접 invoke 시뮬레이션
   - 시나리오: AsyncGenerator yield 3회 → 3 progress events / generator throw → 1 error event / abort 호출 → generator.return() / progress payload zod fail → error event / sender-targeted 검증(다른 webContents에는 안 감)
2. **`tests/unit/renderer/ipc/client-stream.test.ts`** (신규):
   - `window.ipc`를 globalThis stub으로 주입
   - ipcStream 호출 후 streamStart invoke가 일어나고 streamId 반환받는지
   - onStreamEvent 콜백을 직접 invoke해 progress/complete/error 흐름 시뮬레이션
   - 시나리오: progress 콜백 호출 / complete promise resolve / error promise reject / signal abort → cancel + AbortError reject / onProgress unregister 후 호출 안 됨 / 다른 streamId의 이벤트는 무시
3. 두 테스트 모두 `pattern-bun-mock-conventions.md`의 DI-first / leaf-only 규약 준수. real export spread 패턴 사용.
tasks.risk: electron mock이 process-global이면 다른 ipc 테스트 오염 가능 — bun:test scope에서 leaf 모듈만 mock하고 spread real exports로 격리. AsyncGenerator return() 동작 검증은 generator 인스턴스 레퍼런스 보유 후 직접 호출로 가능.

result.summary: Added unit coverage for the ipcStream router primitive and renderer client primitive. Router tests mock Electron and inject a local stream contract descriptor to cover progress, complete, throw/error, cancellation with generator.return(), zod progress validation failure, and sender-targeted delivery. Client tests use an isolated window.ipc bridge registration map to cover start/registration, synchronous progress callbacks, complete/error settlement, AbortSignal cancellation, unregister, and wrong streamId isolation. Targeted bun tests and Biome check pass.

tasks.title: search 마이그레이션: walker → handler → schema → contract → store
tasks.context: "4 results in 0 files" 버그를 ipcStream 위에서 해소. Issue 5의 변경 표 그대로 진행. 5개 모듈을 순차 변경하되, 단일 PR 내부 커밋으로 분리. 각 모듈 변경 후 컴파일 통과 게이트.
tasks.acceptance:
- `bun tsc --noEmit` 통과 (전체 프로젝트).
- `src/renderer/state/stores/search.ts`에서 `requestId`라는 단어가 사라짐.
- `src/main/ipc/channels/fs/search-handlers.ts`에서 `broadcast` import와 호출이 사라짐.
- `src/shared/types/search.ts`의 `SearchProgressSchema`에 `requestId` 필드 없음.
- 기존 search UI 컴포넌트(SearchPanel/SearchInput/SearchResultsList 등) 컴파일 변경 없이 통과.
- biome lint/format 통과.
tasks.risk: walker abort 시멘틱 변경(return → throw)이 walker 단위 테스트의 기대 동작과 충돌 → Task 7에서 함께 갱신. store의 `_storeHelpers` export 제거가 기존 단위 테스트 파일을 깨뜨림 → Task 7에서 재작성. 변경 범위 크지만 의존성이 일방향이라 중간 컴파일 게이트로 안전.

result.summary: Migrated search to ipcStream and completed the revision to align with plan decisions: added walker-level walkAndSearchIter with AbortError-throwing abort semantics, refactored searchTextStream to consume the iterator directly, simplified SearchProgressSchema to FileMatch[] batches, moved fs.searchText from call/listen to stream registration, and updated the renderer search store to use ipcStream with controller-identity stale suppression and no requestId. Verified with bun tsc --noEmit, Biome check on touched search files, and targeted primitive stream tests.

tasks.title: search 채널 단위 테스트 갱신 + round-trip 통합 테스트 신설
tasks.context: Task 6의 마이그레이션 결과를 검증. 기존 helper 직접 호출 단위 테스트(`_storeHelpers.appendBatch`)를 제거하고 ipcStream mock 기반으로 재작성. 핵심: round-trip 통합 테스트가 직전 사이클이 잡지 못한 "0 files" 클래스 버그의 회귀 방지선이 됨.
tasks.acceptance:
- `bun test tests/unit/main/search/walker.test.ts` 통과 (abort 케이스 새 시멘틱 반영).
- `bun test tests/unit/main/ipc/channels/fs/search-handlers.test.ts` 통과 (AsyncGenerator 시퀀스 검증).
- `bun test tests/unit/renderer/state/stores/search.test.ts` 통과 (`_storeHelpers` 사용 없음).
- `bun test tests/unit/shared/types/search.test.ts` 통과.
- `bun test tests/integration/renderer/search/round-trip.test.ts` 통과 — 시나리오 A가 명시적으로 `results.length > 0` 검증.
- 통합 테스트 시나리오 A·B·C·D 모두 통과.
tasks.approach:
### A. 단위 테스트 갱신
1. **`tests/unit/main/search/walker.test.ts`**: abort 케이스를 `await expect(...).rejects.toThrow(/abort/i)`로 변경. 그 외 walker 동작 테스트는 유지.
2. **`tests/unit/main/ipc/channels/fs/search-handlers.test.ts`**: 재작성. 기존 broadcast 호출 mock 검증 → `searchTextStream` AsyncGenerator를 직접 소비해 yield 시퀀스(`FileMatch[]` batches)와 final return value(SearchComplete) 검증. 시나리오: 빈 결과, batch 다수, abort throw, invalid regex throw, workspace not found throw.
3. **`tests/unit/renderer/state/stores/search.test.ts`**: 재작성. `_storeHelpers` 직접 호출 패턴 제거. mock pattern: `mock.module(".../ipc/client", () => ({ ipcStream: mockFn }))`. mockFn은 `{ promise, onProgress }`를 반환하되 promise는 controlled (test가 resolve/reject 가능), onProgress는 등록된 콜백 배열을 노출. 테스트 시나리오: startSearch → onProgress 두 번 호출 시 results 누적 / cancelSearch 시 controller.abort 호출 / 새 startSearch가 prior controller abort.
4. **`tests/unit/shared/types/search.test.ts`**: `SearchProgressSchema` 변경 반영.

### B. Round-trip 통합 테스트 (신규 — 회귀 방지 핵심)
5. **`tests/helpers/ipc-pair.ts`** (신규): in-memory MockWebContents/MockIpcMain pair. send/invoke가 직결. 양쪽이 router(main)와 client(renderer) 양 끝에 부착.
6. **`tests/integration/renderer/search/round-trip.test.ts`** (신규):
   - 시나리오 A — "0 files 회귀 방지": 워크스페이스 fixture(파일 3개에 'needle' 매치 4건)를 만들고 store.startSearch 호출 → router가 streamStart 처리 → 실제 walkAndSearchIter generator 실행 → progress 이벤트가 store까지 도달 → `session.results.length === 3` 확인 (= 3 files). matchesFound === 4. **이 검증이 직전 버그 시나리오를 정확히 reproduce**.
   - 시나리오 B — 동시 다중 stream: 같은 workspace에서 두 startSearch 연속 → 두 번째가 첫 번째를 즉시 abort + 새 stream으로 진행 → 첫 stream의 stale progress가 store에 안 들어감.
   - 시나리오 C — abort: 검색 중간에 cancelSearch → AbortError + 부분 results 보존 + status=idle.
   - 시나리오 D — sender targeting: 두 mock webContents pair가 있을 때 한 pair의 progress가 다른 pair에 도달하지 않음.
tasks.risk: in-memory ipc pair의 정확도가 실제 electron 동작과 100% 일치하지 않을 수 있음 — sender-targeted send 동작이 mock에서도 정확히 흐르도록 webContents 객체 식별성 검증. 통합 테스트가 search 도메인 코드까지 실제로 실행하므로 fixture 디렉토리 생성/정리 책임 필요(tmpdir).

result.summary: Updated search migration tests for ipcStream semantics: walker abort assertions now expect AbortError, searchTextStream handler tests consume AsyncGenerator yields/final return, renderer search store tests mock ipcStream with controlled progress/complete/error/abort flows and no _storeHelpers usage, shared schema/contract tests assert fs.stream.searchText and FileMatch[] progress. Added a focused in-memory IPC pair helper and renderer round-trip integration coverage for the 3-file/4-match regression, concurrent stale progress, abort partial results, and sender targeting. Required targeted tests, bun tsc --noEmit, and Biome check all pass.

tasks.title: 최종 검증 + 메모리 패턴 기록 + 사이클 마무리
tasks.context: 실제 앱에서 search 동작 회복 확인 + 차후 streaming 사용처(find&replace, quick-open, git)가 같은 클래스 버그를 답습하지 않도록 학습 자산 기록. 본 사이클의 마무리 작업.
tasks.acceptance:
- 사용자가 `bun dev`에서 검색이 결과를 제대로 보여준다고 확인.
- `bun test` 전체 통과.
- biome lint/format 통과.
- `.nexus/memory/pattern-ipc-stream.md` 파일 존재 및 ipcStream/subscription/inverted-request 선택 가이드 명확.
- `.nexus/memory/empirical-broadcast-correlation-bug.md` 파일 존재 및 본 lesson 기록.
- `.nexus/context/conventions.md`에 broadcast 상관관계 키 규약 명시.
tasks.approach:
1. **수동 검증** (사용자에게 요청):
   - `bun dev` 실행 → 워크스페이스에서 검색어 입력 → "X results in Y files"의 Y > 0 확인.
   - 큰 워크스페이스에서 검색 중 cancel 동작 확인.
2. **lint/format 통과**: `bun run lint` (biome) / `bun run format` 차이 없음 확인.
3. **`.nexus/memory/pattern-ipc-stream.md` 신설**:
   - "ipcStream vs subscription vs inverted-request 선택 가이드" 한 문서.
   - lifecycle ownership 기준, AsyncGenerator 사용 패턴, streamId 권위 규칙, sender-targeted send 의의.
   - 향후 find&replace/quick-open/git이 참조할 표준.
4. **`.nexus/memory/empirical-broadcast-correlation-bug.md` 신설**:
   - 본 사이클의 lesson: "transport requestId를 도메인 매칭에 쓰면 두 네임스페이스 분리 사고. 단위 테스트가 helper 직접 호출로 우회하면 이 클래스 버그가 통과한다. round-trip 통합 테스트로만 회귀 방지 가능."
5. **`.nexus/context/conventions.md`** (없으면 신설, 있으면 추가):
   - "broadcast 이벤트 상관관계 키는 도메인 식별자(workspaceId/tabId/uri)를 사용한다. transport requestId를 사용하지 않는다." 한 줄.
6. **사이클 close**: 모든 task `nx_task_close` 호출. PR 생성은 사용자 요청 시.
tasks.risk: 사용자 수동 검증이 본 task의 마지막 게이트라 자동화 불가. 메모리 파일은 향후 사용처가 실제로 참조해야 가치 발휘 — 다음 사이클 진입 시 Lead가 검색하도록 의존.

result.summary: Verified the available automated gates and learning artifacts: bun test passed across the suite (1244 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail), bun run typecheck passed, git diff --check passed, Tailwind syntax check passed, Biome check passed for all changed files, the ipcStream and broadcast-correlation memory files exist, and conventions.md contains the broadcast correlation-key rule. Two acceptance items remain not fully satisfied from this CLI run: user manual bun dev search confirmation was not available, and full-repo biome check reports unrelated pre-existing diagnostics outside the touched files, so I did not restyle unrelated code.

tasks.title: Land git contract: shared/types/git.ts + git block in ipc-contract.ts
tasks.context: Issue 4 decision gates everything else: the typed contract surface is added before any handler. Schemas land in `src/shared/types/git.ts` and the `git` channel block is added to `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts`. The exact call/listen/stream split is fixed in the issue 4 decision text — including the two persistence calls (getPanelState, setPanelState) added per issue 9. Reuse `FileContentSchema` from `src/shared/types/fs.ts`.
tasks.acceptance:
- New file `src/shared/types/git.ts` exports all schemas listed above
- `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts` has a top-level `git` block with the 15 calls, 2 listens, 2 streams from issue 4
- `tsc --noEmit` (or `bun run typecheck`) passes; existing tests still pass
- No handler files exist yet — this task is contract-only
tasks.approach: Create `src/shared/types/git.ts` exporting: RepoInfoSchema (discriminated union: detecting | non-repo | repo with gitDir+topLevel), GitStatusEntrySchema (relPath, oldRelPath?, xy two-letter porcelain code), GitStatusSchema (entries grouped merge/staged/working/untracked), BranchInfoSchema (current, upstream?, ahead, behind), BranchListSchema, PullResultSchema, PushResultSchema, DiffSpec discriminated union, LogEntry/LogChunk/LogComplete, DiffChunk/DiffComplete, GitPanelStateSchema (commitDraft + expandedGroups). Then add the `git` block to `src/shared/ipc-contract.ts` exactly per issue 4 decision (15 calls + 2 listens + 2 streams).
tasks.risk: Schema drift later — mitigated by listing every field in the decision text up front. Reuse of FileContentSchema must not duplicate; import directly.

result.summary: Added Git shared schemas/types in src/shared/types/git.ts and wired the top-level git IPC contract in src/shared/ipc-contract.ts with read/write calls, repo/status listens, log/diff streams, and panel-state calls. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck and targeted Biome lint; review confirmed no git handler files were introduced in this contract-only task.

tasks.title: Implement git binary resolver and process runner (git-binary.ts, git-process.ts)
tasks.context: Issue 2 decision: shell out to system git via child_process.spawn. The binary resolver caches a process-level singleton; the process runner exposes runGit (buffered, capped at 10 MB stdout) and streamGit (AsyncIterable of stdout chunks for log/diff). Both honor AbortSignal and spawn with GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 + GIT_ASKPASS=echo + SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE=force + SSH_ASKPASS=echo so push/pull fail fast on missing credentials (issue 8).
tasks.acceptance:
- `git-binary.ts` resolves on macOS/Linux dev box; returns null and does not throw when git is absent
- `runGit` rejects with `GitError` (typed via task 3) when git exits non-zero, with stderr captured
- `runGit` rejects with `AbortError` when signal aborts mid-run; child process is killed
- `streamGit` yields stdout chunks until exit; consumer can break to abort
- 10 MB stdout cap on `runGit` throws typed error, not OOMs
- No tests for "child_process spawns" (Node guarantees) — focus is the contract above
tasks.approach: `src/main/git/git-binary.ts`: `resolveGitBinary()` tries (1) state.gitPath override (no UI in v1, just read the value if present), (2) `which git` / `where git`, (3) `/usr/bin/git` fallback. Returns `{ path, version } | null`. Cached in module scope. `src/main/git/git-process.ts`: `runGit({ bin, cwd, args, env?, signal? })` returns `{ stdout: string, stderr: string, code: number }`. `streamGit(opts)` returns `AsyncIterable<Buffer>` for stdout. Both kill the child on signal abort. Stdout cap at 10 MB throws GitError(kind:"output-too-large").
tasks.risk: Cross-platform binary resolution flakiness on Windows — explicitly noted as v1 best-effort; Windows users without git on PATH see "Git not found" panel notice.

result.summary: Added main-process GitError foundation, git binary resolver, and git process runner/streamer. The implementation resolves the system git binary without throwing when absent, runs git with non-interactive auth env, buffers runGit stdout with a 10 MiB cap, classifies non-zero exits into typed GitError objects, abort-kills child processes, and exposes streamGit as an async generator for log/diff flows. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck, targeted Biome check, and smoke checks for resolver/run/abort/cap/stream break behavior.

tasks.title: Implement GitError + GitRepository (queue, status parser, op methods)
tasks.context: Issue 2 decision: GitRepository owns a serial promise chain — every git invocation, including reads, flows through `queue<T>(fn)` so concurrent checkout cannot tear getFileContent. The status parser consumes `git status --porcelain=v2 -z` output and produces GitStatusSchema-conformant entries grouped by Merge/Index/WorkingTree/Untracked. Ahead/behind comes from the `# branch.ab` header in v2 porcelain.
tasks.acceptance:
- Every public method on GitRepository invokes `runGit`/`streamGit` via `queue` (verified by reading the class)
- `parseV2Porcelain` correctly groups M/A/D/R/C entries into Index, M/D/T into WorkingTree, ? into Untracked, and any conflict cell (DD/AU/UD/UA/DU/AA/UU) into Merge with xy preserved
- Renames produce one entry with both `relPath` and `oldRelPath`
- `# branch.ab +N -M` is parsed into `{ ahead: N, behind: M }`
- `commit("")` rejects with GitError before spawning git
- `dispose()` aborts all queued + in-flight operations
- The class does not import any IPC machinery — it's pure main-side
tasks.approach: `src/main/git/git-error.ts`: `class GitError extends Error { kind: "auth"|"conflict"|"not-repo"|"output-too-large"|"git-missing"|"unknown"; code?: number; stderr?: string; argv?: string[] }`. The auth-kind detection logic lives here as a helper that classifies stderr against the patterns from issue 8. `src/main/git/git-repository.ts`: `class GitRepository { constructor(workspaceId, topLevel, gitDir, bin); private queue<T>(fn): Promise<T>; status(): Promise<GitStatus>; stage(rel: string[]); unstage; discard; commit(message, opts); checkout(ref); createBranch(name, checkout?); listBranches; fetch(remote?); pull(); push(force?); stash(message?); stashPop; getFileContent(ref, relPath); diff(spec, signal): AsyncIterable; log(args, signal): AsyncIterable; refreshStatus(): Promise<void> // runs status + emits via injected broadcast; dispose() }`. Includes `parseV2Porcelain(text: string): GitStatus` exported separately for direct unit testing.
tasks.risk: Porcelain v2 NUL-delimited parsing is fiddly — make `parseV2Porcelain` a pure function over string input so we can run scenario tests against captured fixtures.

result.summary: Refined the typed GitError foundation to the planned kind union and added GitRepository with serialized queued operations, exported parseV2Porcelain, status grouping, branch ahead/behind parsing, repository read/write ops, bounded diff/log streams, getFileContent for git refs/index, and dispose-driven abort handling. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck, targeted Biome check, and ad-hoc smoke checks for parser grouping, real repo operations, and queued/in-flight abort.

tasks.title: Implement git-detect.ts and GitRegistry (per-workspace lazy detection)
tasks.context: Issue 3 decision: lazy detection on first SCM panel activation. `git rev-parse --show-toplevel --git-dir` returns gitDir+topLevel; cached on GitRegistry. Three invalidation triggers: renderer-initiated init call, watcher-observed .git/HEAD creation, explicit refreshDetection. Disposal hooks `wrappedBroadcast` workspace:removed.
tasks.acceptance:
- `getOrDetect` returns the cached repo on second call without spawning git
- `getOrDetect` for a non-repo workspace returns null and caches `{ kind: "non-repo" }`
- `reinit` runs `git init` and flips kind to "repo"
- `dispose(id)` aborts the repo's queue and removes from the map
- `disposeAll` is called from main app `before-quit`
- The registry never imports renderer-side modules
tasks.approach: `src/main/git/git-detect.ts`: `async function detectRepository(root, bin): Promise<RepoInfo>` — runs `git -C <root> rev-parse --show-toplevel --git-dir`, returns `{ kind: "repo", gitDir, topLevel }` on exit-0, `{ kind: "non-repo" }` on exit-128. `src/main/git/git-registry.ts`: `class GitRegistry { constructor(workspaceManager, broadcast, bin); getOrDetect(workspaceId): Promise<GitRepository | null>; reinit(workspaceId): Promise<RepoInfo> // git init + re-detect; refreshDetection(workspaceId): Promise<RepoInfo>; refreshStatus(workspaceId): Promise<void> // calls repo.refreshStatus, emits statusChanged; getRepoInfo(workspaceId): RepoInfo; dispose(workspaceId): void; disposeAll(): void }`. The registry is the single owner of `Map<workspaceId, GitRepository>`; it injects the broadcast fn into each repo so refreshStatus emits through one place.
tasks.risk: Race where panel opens before detection resolves — `getOrDetect` returns a Promise; the channel handler awaits before responding. Initial state in the renderer is `kind: "detecting"` per issue 3.

result.summary: Added git repository detection and GitRegistry ownership. detectRepository wraps rev-parse into repo/non-repo RepoInfo, and GitRegistry caches per-workspace RepoInfo/GitRepository instances, supports lazy getOrDetect, refreshDetection, reinit, refreshStatus broadcasting, and workspace/all disposal while treating missing git as a typed GitError for repository-required calls. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck, targeted Biome check, and fake-git sanity checks for repo/non-repo/caching/reinit/missing-git behavior.

tasks.title: Implement GitWatcher and StatusCoalescer (.git/ watch + per-workspace debounce)
tasks.context: Issue 5 decision: dedicated GitWatcher (separate from FileWatcher) watches gitDir/{HEAD,index,refs,MERGE_HEAD,REBASE_HEAD,CHERRY_PICK_HEAD} via one chokidar instance per repo. Ignore *.lock basenames and objects/, logs/ entirely. All refresh triggers (post-op, watcher dirty events, renderer fs.changed-driven getStatus) converge on a per-workspace StatusCoalescer with 100 ms trailing debounce, which calls `registry.refreshStatus(workspaceId)`.
tasks.acceptance:
- A burst of 50 file events on `.git/refs/remotes/...` produces a single statusChanged broadcast (debounce works)
- Touching `.git/index.lock` does NOT trigger a refresh (ignore works)
- `disposeWorkspace(id)` closes the chokidar instance and cancels the coalescer entry
- Watcher leak test: after dispose, no fs handles remain on the gitDir
- All convergence: a post-op explicit `refreshStatus` followed within 50 ms by a watcher dirty event produces ONE broadcast, not two
tasks.approach: `src/main/git/git-watcher.ts`: `class GitWatcher { constructor(broadcast, onDirty); watch(workspaceId, gitDir); unwatch(workspaceId); disposeWorkspace(workspaceId); dispose(); }` — one chokidar instance per repo with depth:2 rooted at gitDir, ignored: *.lock + objects/ + logs/. On any add/change/unlink, calls `onDirty(workspaceId)`. `src/main/git/status-coalescer.ts`: `function createStatusCoalescer({ delayMs: 100 }): { schedule(workspaceId, runFn): void; cancel(workspaceId): void; clearAll(): void; }` — uses `createKeyedDebouncer` from `src/shared/keyed-debouncer.ts`. Wiring: GitRegistry constructor takes a coalescer; `refreshStatus(id)` schedules with the coalescer; the coalescer's run function calls `repository.refreshStatus()` then `broadcast("git", "statusChanged", { workspaceId, status })`.
tasks.risk: Chokidar depth:2 may miss `.git/refs/remotes/origin/main` if structure deepens — verify in scenario test on a real fetched repo. If miss, switch to depth:Infinity with explicit ignore.

result.summary: Added GitWatcher and StatusCoalescer. GitWatcher maintains one chokidar watcher per workspace gitDir, ignores lock/object/log noise, and emits dirty callbacks for status-relevant add/change/unlink events. createStatusCoalescer provides trailing per-workspace debounce and collapses triggers during an active refresh into exactly one follow-up run. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck, targeted Biome check, and ad-hoc checks for coalescer burst/follow-up behavior plus ignore helper behavior.

tasks.title: Add WorkspaceStorage migration v3 and git_panel_state accessors
tasks.context: Issue 9 decision: persist exactly two pieces of per-workspace state — commitDraft (string) and expandedGroups (JSON of {merge,staged,working,untracked}: boolean) — in a single kv table `git_panel_state`. Migration is added to `WORKSPACE_DB_MIGRATIONS` in `src/main/storage/workspace-storage.ts` as version 3. Accessors mirror `getExpandedPaths`/`setExpandedPaths`.
tasks.acceptance:
- Existing workspace DBs (schemaVersion 2) auto-migrate to v3 on next open
- `getGitPanelState` on a fresh workspace returns `{ commitDraft: "", expandedGroups: { merge: true, staged: true, working: true, untracked: true } }`
- `setGitPanelState({ commitDraft: "WIP" })` persists across `closeForWorkspace` + `openForWorkspace`
- Corrupt expandedGroups JSON in the DB does not throw — accessor logs and returns defaults
- Existing migrations (v1, v2) remain unmodified per the file's comment "Once shipped, do NOT edit"
tasks.approach: In `src/main/storage/workspace-storage.ts` append migration v3: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS git_panel_state (key TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL)`. Add two methods on WorkspaceStorage: `getGitPanelState(workspaceId): { commitDraft: string; expandedGroups: ExpandedGroups }` (returns defaults when rows absent), and `setGitPanelState(workspaceId, partial: Partial<...>)` — uses INSERT OR REPLACE per key, in a transaction. Validate the JSON via the GitPanelStateSchema from task 9 on read so corrupt rows fall back to defaults rather than throwing.
tasks.risk: v2 migration's transaction pattern (BEGIN/COMMIT around prepared statements) must be matched exactly — the file's existing comment block is the contract.

result.summary: Appended WorkspaceStorage migration v3 for git_panel_state and added getGitPanelState/setGitPanelState accessors using shared Git panel schemas/defaults. Fresh workspaces return default panel state, partial updates persist as key/value rows, and corrupt expandedGroups JSON logs a warning and falls back to defaults without throwing. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck, targeted Biome lint, diff whitespace check, and an ad-hoc SQLite behavior check for defaults/migration/persistence/corruption.

tasks.title: Implement read/state git channel handlers (getRepoInfo, init, getStatus, getFileContent, listBranches, getPanelState, setPanelState)
tasks.context: Issue 4 decision: handlers live in `src/main/ipc/channels/git/` split by concern. This task covers the read-side and panel-state handlers. They all delegate to GitRegistry / GitRepository / WorkspaceStorage and validate args via the contract schemas. getFileContent caps at 10 MB, runs binary detection (existing src/main/filesystem/binary-detect.ts), returns `{ binary: true }` placeholder for binaries.
tasks.acceptance:
- Each handler validates args with the contract schema and rejects malformed payloads with a uniform error
- `getStatus` for a non-repo workspace returns the empty-groups status, not an error
- `getFileContent` for a 12 MB file rejects with GitError(kind:"output-too-large"); renderer-visible message is "File too large to diff"
- `getFileContent` for a known-binary file returns `{ binary: true, size }` without spawning git show
- `getPanelState` returns sensible defaults for fresh workspaces
- `setPanelState({ commitDraft: "..." })` round-trips through the DB
- Handlers do not import renderer modules; they use the broadcast already injected into the registry
tasks.approach: Create `src/main/ipc/channels/git/index.ts` with `registerGitChannel(registry, storage)` that calls `register("git", { call, listen, stream })`. Split handlers across files mirroring the fs channel pattern: `repo-handlers.ts` (getRepoInfo, init), `status-handlers.ts` (getStatus), `content-handlers.ts` (getFileContent — runs through repository.getFileContent, applies size cap and binary check), `branch-handlers.ts` (listBranches — read side only), `state-handlers.ts` (getPanelState, setPanelState — wrap WorkspaceStorage accessors, validate via GitPanelStateSchema).
tasks.risk: Binary detection on git-show output requires reading the buffer first — perform the cap check before binary-detect to avoid loading 100 MB into memory.

result.summary: Added git IPC read/state channel registration and handlers for getRepoInfo, init, getStatus, getFileContent, listBranches, getPanelState, and setPanelState. Handlers validate with shared contract schemas, delegate to GitRegistry/GitRepository/WorkspaceStorage, return empty status/branch shapes for non-repos, and convert git blob content into FileContent with binary probing. Verified by the engineer with targeted Biome check and bun run typecheck. Write/op handlers and log/diff streams remain for task 16.

tasks.title: Implement write/op git channel handlers and streams (stage, unstage, discard, commit, checkout, createBranch, fetch, pull, push, stash, stashPop, log, diff)
tasks.context: Issue 4 decision: write handlers and the two streams. Every write handler calls the corresponding GitRepository method and then `registry.refreshStatus(workspaceId)` BEFORE returning so the statusChanged broadcast lands ahead of the call resolution (issue 5 decision). pull/push return typed results; auth failures from issue 8 surface as GitError(kind:"auth"). log and diff are async generators per the existing fs.searchText stream pattern.
tasks.acceptance:
- After `commit`, the renderer receives a `git.statusChanged` event before the call's promise resolves (verified by ordering trace)
- `push` against a remote requiring auth resolves with GitError(kind:"auth") and stderr-derived message; the panel surfaces "Open Terminal" CTA per issue 8
- `discardChanges` for an untracked file deletes it (`git clean -f -- <relPath>`) and refreshes
- `log` stream is cancellable mid-iteration via AbortSignal — the child git process is killed, no zombies
- `diff` stream emits DiffChunk batches no larger than 1 MB each (back-pressure)
- Validation errors from contract schemas are converted to typed errors uniformly
tasks.approach: Files in `src/main/ipc/channels/git/`: `stage-handlers.ts` (stage, unstage, discardChanges), `commit-handlers.ts` (commit), `branch-handlers.ts` (checkout, createBranch — write side; listBranches stays in task 14's read file), `sync-handlers.ts` (fetch, pull, push), `stash-handlers.ts` (stash, stashPop), `log-stream.ts` (log async generator — `git log --pretty=format:... -z` consumer, yields LogChunk batches, returns LogComplete), `diff-stream.ts` (diff async generator — consumes streamGit output for `git diff`/`git diff --cached`/`git show`, yields DiffChunk batches). All write handlers end with `await registry.refreshStatus(workspaceId)` before returning.
tasks.risk: discardChanges semantics differ for staged vs. unstaged vs. untracked files — must dispatch on current status to invoke the right git verb (checkout/restore for tracked, clean for untracked). Document in stage-handlers.ts comment.

result.summary: Added main-side write/op git IPC handlers for stage, unstage, discardChanges, commit, checkout, createBranch, fetch, pull, push, stash, and stashPop. Each successful mutation delegates to GitRepository and awaits registry.refreshStatus(workspaceId) before returning, preserving statusChanged-before-resolution ordering. Added log/diff stream handlers that wrap repository streams with ctx.signal cancellation. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck and targeted Biome check; Lead re-ran typecheck and targeted Biome after integration wiring.

tasks.title: Wire GitRegistry + GitWatcher into main/index.ts and register git channel
tasks.context: Tie all main-side pieces together: build resolveGitBinary at app.whenReady, instantiate StatusCoalescer + GitWatcher + GitRegistry with the wrappedBroadcast, register the git channel, and ensure workspace:removed cleanup hooks GitWatcher and GitRegistry exactly like FileWatcher does today.
tasks.acceptance:
- App starts with `git` not on PATH and panel renders a "Git not found" notice (no crash)
- App starts with git on PATH and SCM panel works end-to-end
- Removing a workspace immediately disposes its GitWatcher and any in-flight GitRepository operations (verified by no lingering chokidar handles, no orphaned child processes)
- `before-quit` cleanly shuts down all registries and watchers
- No double-registration of the git channel on hot reload — register is "last wins" but should be called exactly once per process
tasks.approach: In `src/main/index.ts`: extend `wrappedBroadcast` to also call `gitWatcher.disposeWorkspace(removedWorkspaceId)` and `gitRegistry.dispose(removedWorkspaceId)` on `workspace:removed`, in the same block where `fileWatcher.disposeWorkspace` is called. After `app.whenReady()`, `await resolveGitBinary()`, instantiate the coalescer/watcher/registry, call `registerGitChannel(gitRegistry, workspaceStorage)`. Add `gitRegistry.disposeAll(); gitWatcher.dispose();` to the existing `app.on("before-quit", ...)` block. The git binary, if not found, leaves the registry in a "git-missing" mode where every call returns GitError(kind:"git-missing") — no panel-only branch needed.
tasks.risk: The wrappedBroadcast pattern is currently single-purpose for FileWatcher; expanding it must keep the if-blocks orthogonal so a future fourth subscriber doesn't tangle them. Keep each domain in its own `if (channelName === "workspace" && event === "removed")` block.

result.summary: Wired main-process Git lifecycle into src/main/index.ts: resolved git binary at app readiness, created StatusCoalescer, GitWatcher, and GitRegistry, registered the git IPC channel, hooked repoInfoChanged into watcher registration, scheduled status refreshes from watcher dirty events, and disposed registry/watcher/coalescer on workspace removal and before-quit. Added a small GitRegistry onRepoInfoChanged callback option to support watcher wiring without coupling the registry to chokidar. Verified with bun run typecheck and targeted Biome check on main/index, git-registry, and git channel files.

tasks.title: Implement renderer useGitStore (zustand) with statusChanged subscription and workspace-cleanup hookup
tasks.context: Issue 7 decision: `useGitStore` mirrors `useSearchStore`'s session-map shape, keyed by workspaceId. Subscribes once at module init to `git.statusChanged` and `git.repoInfoChanged`, fans out by workspaceId. Registers `closeAllForWorkspace` with `registerWorkspaceCleanup`. Holds: repoInfo, status, branchInfo, commitDraft (debounced-persisted via setPanelState), expandedGroups (persisted on toggle), inFlightOp (commit/push/pull/...), lastError. Selectors expose the current workspace's session.
tasks.acceptance:
- Switching workspaces does not re-fetch status if a session already exists for the target workspace (cache hit)
- Removing a workspace clears its session entirely and aborts any in-flight op
- Typing in the commit input debounces persistence — 5 keystrokes within 500 ms produce 1 setPanelState call
- An in-flight op is reflected in the UI via `inFlightOp` and cleared on resolution
- A `git.statusChanged` event for an unmounted workspace is dropped silently (broadcast-and-filter)
- `useGitSession(workspaceId)` is a stable selector hook that returns the session or undefined
tasks.approach: `src/renderer/state/stores/git.ts`: `interface GitSession { repoInfo, status, statusFetching, branchInfo, commitDraft, expandedGroups, inFlightOp, lastError }`. Module-scope `controllers = new Map<workspaceId, AbortController>()` for in-flight ops (mirrors search.ts). Module-init subscribes to `window.ipc.on("git", "statusChanged", ...)` and applies the new status to the matching session. On first activation per workspace, dispatch `getRepoInfo` + `getStatus` + `getPanelState` in parallel to seed. Actions: `loadInitial(workspaceId)`, `refresh(workspaceId)`, `setCommitDraft(workspaceId, text)` (debounced 500 ms persist via setPanelState), `setExpandedGroup(workspaceId, group, expanded)` (persist immediately), `stage/unstage/discard/commit/...` (each runs the call, sets inFlightOp during, clears + sets lastError on outcome), `closeAllForWorkspace(workspaceId)`. Register cleanup with registerWorkspaceCleanup at store creation, exactly like useSearchStore does.
tasks.risk: commitDraft persistence timing — the 500 ms debounce must flush on workspace switch and on app close-attempt; otherwise the user's last keystrokes vanish. Add a flush on visibilitychange or window blur.

result.summary: Added renderer useGitStore/useGitSession with per-workspace sessions, module-level git.statusChanged/repoInfoChanged fan-out, workspace cleanup, commit draft debounce + flush hooks, panel-state persistence, and inFlightOp-tracked actions for the planned git operations. Verified by the engineer with bun run typecheck and targeted Biome check; end-to-end exercise is deferred until main handlers are wired.

tasks.title: Build Source Control panel UI components (GitPanel and 13 sub-components)

Reuse existing tokens (text-app-ui-sm, text-app-body, text-muted-foreground, bg-frosted-veil, bg-frosted-veil-strong, border-mist-border, text-warning/success/destructive, etc.). tasks.title: Add editor.diff tab subtype and Monaco DiffEditor tab implementation
tasks.context: Issue 6 decision: extend TabMeta with a discriminated `editor.diff` subtype. Diff tab fetches left via git.getFileContent, right via fs.readFile (or git.getFileContent for INDEX). Subscribes to fs.changed + git.statusChanged for live update. Per-side version tokens reject stale responses. Lazy-load the Monaco DiffEditor.
tasks.acceptance:
- Clicking a changed file row in the SCM panel opens a Monaco DiffEditor in a new tab with correct left/right content
- Editing the working-tree file from outside (e.g. another editor) updates the right pane within ~200 ms (debounced fs.changed)
- Committing the staged change shifts the diff: file moves out of the SCM panel; if the diff tab was index-vs-head, it recomputes
- Renamed files render with `oldRelPath → relPath` in the tab title and load HEAD content from oldRelPath
- A 12 MB file shows "File too large to diff" instead of crashing the editor
- A binary file shows a placeholder, not Monaco, and reports file size
- Closing the workspace closes the diff tab via existing closeAllForWorkspace
tasks.approach: Update `src/shared/types/tab.ts`: TabMeta union becomes a discriminated union on `type`, with `"editor.diff"` carrying `DiffTabPayload = { workspaceId; relPath; leftRef; rightRef; oldRelPath? }`. Existing tab.create call args may need a payload field (or a new method `createDiffTab`). Update `src/renderer/state/operations/tabs.ts` with `openDiffTab(workspaceId, relPath, leftRef, rightRef, oldRelPath?)` that creates the tab and routes a click. Create `src/renderer/components/editor/diff-tab.tsx` consuming `@monaco-editor/react`'s DiffEditor; create `src/renderer/components/editor/diff-content-loader.ts` exporting `useDiffContent(workspaceId, relPath, leftRef, rightRef): { left, right, status, reload }` that subscribes to fs.changed + git.statusChanged via the existing IPC client and uses per-side version tokens. The workspace-panel's tab renderer must dispatch on `tab.type === "editor.diff"`.
tasks.risk: TabMeta is currently a flat shape; turning it into a discriminated union may require changes to the tabs store and existing renderers — verify all call sites of `tab.type === "editor"` etc. still narrow correctly. Bun's TS server can flag mismatches.

result.summary: Added the editor.diff tab subtype, openDiffTab transaction, lazy DiffEditor renderer, diff-content loader with git/fs content fetching and change subscriptions, and workspace content/group handling for diff tabs. Verified with typecheck, targeted Biome, lint:tw, and targeted tab/content tests in the implementation pass.
